rail.subtitleCreate: A project is a single billable app or website. You can add more platforms later.
rail.subtitleUpdate: Each step is independently saveable. Changes to platform credentials trigger a re-import.
rail.tipBody: You can leave platforms empty for now and complete them after creation. Changes here never delete revenue history.

basics.title: Project basics
basics.description: Give the project a name and a short description. You can edit both at any time from project settings.
basics.name: Project name
basics.nameHint: Shown in dashboards, exports, and webhook payloads. {{count}}/{{max}}.
basics.descriptionPlaceholder: What this project tracks, who owns it, any special handling…

platforms.title: Pick your app platforms
platforms.description: Choose every billing surface that earns money for this project. Each platform requires its own credentials — you can paste them now or skip and add later.

ios.name: iOS — App Store
ios.desc: StoreKit, App Store Connect API. Auto-imports subscriptions, IAPs, and family-share data.

android.name: Android — Play Store
android.desc: Play Billing v6+, Google Play Developer API. Auto-imports SKUs and grace periods.

stripe.name: Stripe
stripe.desc: Stripe checkout, customer portal, billing webhooks. Idempotent transactional imports.

currency.title: Currency & timezone
currency.description: These settings control how revenue is normalized and how time-bucketed metrics roll up. They can be changed later, but historical reports will be re-aggregated.
currency.reportingCurrencyHint: All MRR/ARR/revenue figures are converted to this currency using daily rates.
currency.fxSourceHint: Fixed to the European Central Bank — daily reference rates, free and self-hosted.

ecb.name: ECB
ecb.desc: European Central Bank · daily reference rates · free

currency.timezoneHint: Daily/weekly/monthly buckets snap to this zone.

review.descriptionCreate: Double-check the configuration. After you create the project you'll be taken to the team page to invite your collaborators.
review.descriptionUpdate: Double-check the configuration. Modified sections will save independently.

auth.title: Sign in to Rovenue
auth.subtitle: Continue with GitHub or Google to manage your projects.

settings.webhookSecretConfigured: A webhook signing secret is currently configured. Rotating generates a new one and invalidates the old value immediately.
settings.webhookSecretMissing: No webhook secret is set. Rotate to generate one.
settings.dangerZoneDescription: Deleting a project is permanent and cannot be undone.

settingsForm.webhookUrlDescription: Outgoing webhooks post here. Leave blank to disable delivery.

delete.body: This action is permanent. All subscribers, purchases, API keys, and webhook configuration for <0>{{name}}</0> will be removed.
delete.confirmLabel: Type the project name to confirm

rotateSecret.body: This invalidates the current secret immediately. Any webhook delivery signed with the old secret will fail verification until you update the receiving endpoint.
rotateSecret.copyBody: This is the only time we'll show the secret. Store it in your deployment secrets before closing this dialog.

search.placeholder: Search by user, event type, product, country, txn id…

empty.title: No events match
empty.body: Try clearing filters or widening your search.

detail.emptyBody: Click any row in the stream to inspect its full payload, timeline and related records.

products.title: Products
products.subtitle: Subscriptions, one-time purchases and consumables across all stores

empty.title: No products match
empty.body: Try widening your filters or creating a new product.

drawer.regionalNote: Regional pricing, tax handling and promo offers are inherited from the {{group}} product group.

productGroups.title: Product groups
productGroups.subtitle: Bundles of products that share entitlements. Offerings pick which products to show on a paywall.

products.heading: Products in this group
products.subtitle: Subscribers who buy any product below enter this group.

empty.title: No products linked yet
empty.body: Link existing products from your catalog to grant this group's entitlements on purchase.

removeProduct.title: Remove product from group?
removeProduct.subtitle: The product stays in your catalog. Existing subscribers keep their access; new buyers won't enter this group.

errors.generic: Could not remove the product. Please try again.

empty.title: No product groups yet
empty.body: Create a product group to bundle SKUs that grant the same entitlements.

empty.allLinked: All products are already in this group
empty.hint: Inactive products are hidden. Create a product in the catalog first.

errors.generic: Could not link the selected products. Please try again.

form.createSubtitle: Bundle SKUs that grant the same entitlements. You can link products afterwards.
form.editSubtitle: Update the group's name, identifier or description. Linked products stay attached.

name.label: Name
name.hint: Shown across the dashboard and on paywalls.

identifier.label: Identifier
identifier.hint: Stable key referenced by the SDK and webhooks. Changing it will break existing client integrations.
identifier.invalid: Use lowercase letters, numbers, hyphens or underscores.
identifier.taken: An existing group already uses this identifier.

description.label: Description
description.placeholder: What does this group unlock for the user?
description.hint: Optional context for teammates browsing the catalog.

default.label: Set as default group
default.hint: New subscribers fall into this group when no other group matches.

errors.generic: Could not save the group. Please try again.

delete.title: Delete product group
delete.subtitle: Permanently remove this group. Linked products will not be deleted.
delete.defaultWarning: This is the default group. New subscribers without a matching group will no longer fall back here.

errors.generic: Could not delete the group. Please try again.

subscribers.title: Subscribers
subscribers.subtitle: End users with at least one purchase, trial, or granted entitlement on {{project}} · Production.

transactions.title: Transactions
transactions.subtitle: Every paid, refunded, or disputed event across App Store, Google Play, Stripe, and web. One row per store transaction.

flow.title: Revenue flow · last 28 days
flow.subtitle: From gross receipts to realized net, by platform
flow.awaitingStores: Per-store revenue will appear here once events ship.

subscriptions.title: Subscriptions
subscriptions.subtitle: Every billing relationship — one row per subscription, not per user. Track renewals, grace, billing retries.

grant.title: Grant subscription
grant.subtitle: Manually provision a subscription for a subscriber.

errors.datePast: Date must be in the future.

composition.title: Subscription composition
composition.subtitle: {{total}} subscriptions across iOS, Google Play, Stripe

calendar.title: Renewal calendar
calendar.subtitle: Upcoming renewals, trials converting, grace periods ending.

filters.searchPlaceholder: Search subscription ID, user, transaction ID

table.empty: No subscriptions match the current filters.
table.footerHint: Click any row to inspect lifecycle, billing, and grants.

billingIssues.title: Billing issues
billingIssues.subtitle: Subscriptions in retry, grace, or pending manual review

schedule.title: Schedule cancel ({{count}})
schedule.storeNote: App Store and Play Store cancellations are requested via webhook; the final state will sync from the store.

featureFlags.title: Feature flags
featureFlags.subtitle: Remote-configure behavior without shipping an update. Rules evaluate top-down on each SDK check; changes propagate in under 30 seconds via the edge cache.

search.placeholder: Search flag key, description, or tag…

kill.title: Kill switch active
kill.body: All rules bypassed. Returning <0>false</0> to every caller until re-enabled.

new.title: New feature flag
new.subtitle: Pick a stable key the SDK checks against. Rules evaluate top-down; the default value is returned when no rule matches.

sections.basicsSub: Identify the flag, pick its environment, and choose whether it ships in the on or off state.
sections.typeSub: The default value is served when no targeting rule matches.
sections.rulesSub: Conditions on the same rule are AND-combined. Empty rules match every subscriber.

descriptions.key: Lowercase letters, digits, dashes or underscores. The SDK looks this up verbatim.
descriptions.description: Optional context shown in the dashboard and audit log.
descriptions.enabled: When off the flag short-circuits to the default value for every caller.
descriptions.env: Pick the environment this flag belongs to. The SDK fetches flags by env, so prod stays isolated from staging and dev.
descriptions.defaultValue: Returned when no rule matches the request.
descriptions.conditions: Subscribers must satisfy every condition for this rule to apply.
descriptions.rollout: Fraction of matched subscribers that receive the value. Hashed deterministically on user id.
descriptions.ruleValue: Value returned when this rule matches and falls within the rollout.

placeholders.description: What does this flag control, who owns it, when can it be retired…

typeDescriptions.boolean: true / false. Best for kill switches and gated features.
typeDescriptions.string: Arbitrary string — variant ids, copy keys, layout names.
typeDescriptions.number: Numeric value — limits, thresholds, sample rates.
typeDescriptions.json: Structured value — config objects, nested settings.

errors.invalidKey: Key must contain only letters, digits, dashes or underscores.
errors.invalidJsonDefault: Default value is not valid JSON: {{error}}
errors.invalidJsonRule: Rule {{index}}: value is not valid JSON ({{error}})
errors.unknown: Something went wrong. Please try again.

edit.title: Edit feature flag
edit.subtitle: Update the default value, targeting rules, or description. The key and type are locked once a flag exists.
edit.notFound: We couldn't find that feature flag.
edit.keyLocked: Key is permanent — create a new flag to rename.
edit.typeLocked: Type is permanent — create a new flag to switch.

experiments.title: Experiments
experiments.subtitle: Run A/B and multi-arm tests on pricing, paywalls, and onboarding. Traffic allocation is deterministic on

actions.deleteConfirm: Delete experiment "{{name}}"? This can only be done while the experiment is still a draft, and cannot be undone.
actions.deleteFailed: Could not delete experiment. It may already be running or completed.
actions.duplicateFailed: Could not duplicate experiment. Please try again.

detail.comingSoon: Detailed analytics (variants, funnel, timeline) ship in Phase 3 — once results are aggregated for this experiment they'll appear here.

new.title: New experiment
new.subtitle: Allocation is deterministic on hash(user_id). Variant weights must sum to 1.

sections.basicsSub: Give the experiment a recognizable name and a stable identifier.
sections.targetingSub: Pick what the experiment changes and who sees it.
sections.variantsSub: At least two arms. Weights are deterministic on hash(user_id) and must sum to 1.

descriptions.name: Shown in the experiments list and reports. Keep it short.
descriptions.key: Lowercase letters, digits, dashes or underscores. The SDK uses this to look up the experiment — change it later and existing assignments are lost.
descriptions.description: Why you're running it and what success looks like.
descriptions.audience: Defaults to the project's default audience. Pick a narrower segment to restrict who is enrolled.
descriptions.variantId: Stable id used in code and analytics — don't change after launch.
descriptions.variantWeight: Share of eligible users that get this arm. Moving one slider redistributes the rest so the total always stays at 100%.
descriptions.variantValue: JSON value the SDK returns when this arm is picked. Use the wand to pretty-print.
descriptions.productGroup: Identifier the SDK loads when this arm is picked. Each arm should point at a different group.

typeDescriptions.flag: Return a typed flag value (boolean, string, number) — best for on/off and copy tests.
typeDescriptions.product_group: Swap the product group identifier the SDK loads — use for price and product mix tests.
typeDescriptions.paywall: Ship a structured paywall config (title, CTA, layout) as remote config.
typeDescriptions.element: Return an arbitrary JSON blob — flexible escape hatch for one-off UI tests.

placeholders.name: Higher entry price on the post-onboarding paywall
placeholders.variantValue: JSON value the SDK should return

new.audienceEmpty: No audiences in this project yet

errors.invalidKey: Key must contain only letters, digits, - or _.
errors.invalidJson: Variant value must be valid JSON: {{message}}
errors.productGroupMissing: Pick a product group for every variant.

subtypeDescriptions.boolean: Each arm returns true or false — the simplest on/off test.
subtypeDescriptions.string: Each arm returns a string — good for copy or enum-style choices.
subtypeDescriptions.number: Each arm returns a number — useful for limits, prices, or thresholds.

valueDescriptions.boolean: Boolean value this arm returns from useFlag().
valueDescriptions.string: String value this arm returns from useFlag().
valueDescriptions.number: Number value this arm returns from useFlag().

productGroup.noneTitle: You don't have any product groups yet. Create at least two before you can run a product-group experiment.

edit.title: Edit experiment
edit.subtitle: Tweak targeting, variants and traffic weights. Editable while the experiment is still a draft — once live, use the lifecycle controls.
edit.notFound: Experiment not found, or you don't have access to it.

readOnlyBanner.running: This experiment is live. Configuration is locked — pause or complete it from the lifecycle bar before editing.
readOnlyBanner.paused: This experiment is paused. Resume it to keep collecting data, or complete it to lock the result. Configuration is read-only.
readOnlyBanner.completed: This experiment is completed. Configuration is locked — duplicate it to run another iteration.

lifecycle.stopConfirm: Complete experiment "{{name}}"? Traffic will stop and the result will be locked in.

winnerBanner.sub: Est. +{{lift}}% lift on {{metric}}. CI excludes zero. Safe to ship.

cumulative.title: Cumulative {{metric}}
cumulative.subtitle: Daily cumulative rate per variant — shaded region is 95% CI band

paid.title: Trial → paid
paid.sub: Converted to paid sub after trial

guardrail.title: Guardrail alert: refund rate
guardrail.sub: Variant A +12% refund rate · dismissed, within tolerance

sample.title: Sample size target reached
sample.sub: 9k+ per variant · continuing for seasonality control

latest.title: Latest reading
latest.sub: Variant B leading at 64% confidence · 80% target

trialStartRate.name: Trial start rate
trialStartRate.description: Share of users who start a trial after viewing the onboarding paywall.

cohorts.title: Cohorts
cohorts.subtitle: Group subscribers by behavior, acquisition channel, or lifecycle stage. Compare retention, revenue, and LTV across segments — then sync cohorts to ad platforms or use them as targeting rules.

mockBadge.label: Mock
mockBadge.tooltip: Backend not wired yet — showing placeholder data

sync.title: Use this cohort
sync.subtitle: Sync to destinations or target from experiments & flags

credits.title: Credits
credits.subtitle: Virtual-currency ledger. Every grant, purchase, and consumption is double-entry; balances reconcile to the second. Use this view to monitor liability, package mix, and burn-rate per feature.

volume.title: Credit volume · last 28 days
volume.subtitle: Daily issuance vs burn — net positive means liability is growing

ledger.title: Ledger · live
ledger.empty: No credit movements match this filter.

liability.title: Outstanding liability
liability.reserveBody: paid liability moved <0>{{delta}}</0> vs prev window. Suggested reserve <1>{{amount}}</1>.
liability.reserveBodyFlat: Suggested reserve <0>{{amount}}</0> for the paid liability.

grant.title: Grant credits
grant.subtitle: Append a manual entry to a subscriber's credit ledger.

fields.descriptionPlaceholder: Why is this credit being granted?

charts.title: Charts
charts.subtitle: Pre-built revenue, retention, and conversion charts. Pick from the library on the left, slice with filters on the right, save as a view, or pin to the dashboard. Every chart is a SQL query under the hood — open in Queries to edit.

catalog.deleteConfirm: Delete "{{name}}"? This affects the whole project.

mrr.error: Couldn't load MRR — using sample series.

newChart.title: New chart
newChart.subtitle: Adds a chart to the project's shared catalog. Pin filters and group-by after it's created.

name.label: Name
name.hint: Shown in the catalog and on the chart card.

description.label: Description
description.hint: Optional context for teammates browsing the library.

errors.generic: Could not create the chart. Please try again.

profile.title: Profile
profile.subtitle: This is how you appear in Rovenue. Some fields are visible to your teammates.

photo.title: Photo & display
photo.subtitle: Your avatar shows up in comments, audit logs, and the activity feed.
photo.hint: PNG, JPG, or GIF · max 2 MB · 240×240 recommended

info.title: Personal information
info.subtitle: Your legal name is used on invoices and tax documents.
info.bioCount: {{count}}/280 · Visible to teammates in your profile card.

contact.title: Contact
contact.subtitle: Where we reach you for security alerts and account recovery.
contact.emailHint: Changing your email requires confirmation from both addresses.

locale.title: Locale & timezone
locale.subtitle: Affects only your view — team-wide reporting timezone is set per project.

activity.title: Activity & visibility
activity.profileVisibleDesc: Your name, avatar, and bio appear in shared dashboards.
activity.statusVisibleDesc: Lets teammates see when you're viewing the same dashboard.

security.title: Security
security.subtitle: Authentication, devices, and recovery options for your account.

password.title: Password
password.subtitle: Choose a strong password unique to Rovenue. Minimum 12 characters.

twofa.title: Two-factor authentication
twofa.subtitle: Add a second step at sign-in. Required for owners on production projects.

authenticator.title: Authenticator app
authenticator.desc: Use 1Password, Authy, or Google Authenticator to generate codes. Configured Apr 2.

hardware.title: Hardware security key
hardware.desc: YubiKey, Titan, or any FIDO2 key. Recommended for highest assurance.

sms.title: SMS backup
sms.desc: Code via text to {{phone}}. Avoid if your phone supports an authenticator.

recovery.title: Recovery codes
recovery.desc: 10 single-use codes for when you can't access 2FA. {{remaining}} of 10 remaining.

sessions.title: Active sessions
sessions.subtitle: Devices currently signed in. Revoke any you don't recognize.

notifications.title: Notifications
notifications.subtitle: Choose what we ping you about and where it lands.

channels.title: Channels
channels.subtitle: Where notifications are delivered. Critical security alerts always go to email.

billing.title: Billing & operational
billing.subtitle: Account-level notifications you usually want on.

marketing.title: Marketing
marketing.subtitle: Optional. We won't share your email.

push.title: Mobile push
push.desc: Rovenue iOS app on iPhone 15 Pro

daily_digest.title: Daily digest
daily_digest.desc: Yesterday's MRR, new subs, churn at 9:00 in your timezone.

weekly_summary.title: Weekly summary
weekly_summary.desc: Sent Mondays. Full revenue + cohort recap.

anomaly.title: Anomaly detection
anomaly.desc: Real-time alert when MRR, churn, or refunds deviate >3σ from forecast.

churn_spike.title: Churn spike
churn_spike.desc: When daily churn exceeds 1.5× the 7-day rolling average.

low_balance.title: Credit balance low
low_balance.desc: When your account credit drops below $50.

product_news.title: Product news
product_news.desc: Monthly product updates and changelog highlights.

connected.title: Connected accounts
connected.subtitle: Sign-in providers and third-party apps with access to your Rovenue account.

providers.title: Sign-in methods
providers.subtitle: You can sign in to Rovenue with any of the following.

apps.title: OAuth apps with access
apps.subtitle: Third-party apps you've authorized. Revoke anytime.

billing.title: Billing & plan
billing.subtitle: Manage your subscription, payment method, and billing address.

plan.name: Scale
plan.description: Up to 50,000 tracked subscribers · 10M events/mo · Unlimited dashboards · Priority support

payment.title: Payment method
payment.subtitle: All cards are tokenized — we never see the full PAN.

details.title: Billing details
details.subtitle: Used on every invoice. Make sure these match your tax records.

invoices.title: Invoices
invoices.subtitle: All Rovenue subscription and usage invoices.

usage.title: Usage
usage.subtitle: Your current billing period · May 1 – May 8, 2026 (8 of 31 days).

limits.title: Plan limits
limits.subtitle: Hard caps trigger overage billing at $0.05 per 10k events. Soft caps are alerts only.

api.title: API & webhooks
api.subtitle: Personal API keys for scripts and integrations. For project-level keys see Project → API.

tokens.title: Personal access tokens
tokens.subtitle: Tokens inherit your account permissions. Rotate every 90 days.

local.name: Local dev
local.meta: Created Jan 9 · last used 6 days ago

placeholder.title: Workspace team
placeholder.body: See Workspace → Team for invitations and roles.

appearance.title: Appearance
appearance.subtitle: Local preferences for how Rovenue looks on this device.

dark.name: Dark
dark.desc: Default. Lower eye strain at night.

export.title: Export & danger zone
export.subtitle: Download your account data, or close your account.

data.title: Export account data
data.subtitle: Includes profile, preferences, comments, saved charts, and audit log entries.
data.hint: You'll receive a download link by email within 24 hours. Project-level data is exported separately from each project.

transfer.title: Transfer account ownership
transfer.subtitle: Move ownership of all your projects to another team member.

close.title: Close account
close.subtitle: Permanently delete your Rovenue account, all personal data, and revoke all sessions. Project data is retained if you have co-owners.
close.warning: This is irreversible. Once closed, your account cannot be recovered. Active subscriptions will be canceled and prorated. You'll have 30 days to download exports before they're purged.

queries.title: Queries
queries.subtitle: SQL workbench against your Rovenue data warehouse — saved queries, scheduled refreshes, and downloadable CSV/Parquet exports.

apps.title: Apps & integrations
apps.subtitle: Connect Rovenue to attribution, ad networks, analytics, data warehouses, and lifecycle tools — server-side, signed, and replayable.

hero.title: Stream every revenue event, everywhere it matters.
hero.description: {{count}} prebuilt integrations across attribution, ads, CDPs, warehouses, lifecycle, and automation. All deliveries are HMAC-signed, retried automatically for 24h, and replayable from the dashboard.

rail.consoleHelp: Don't see your tool? Build a custom integration with our REST API or webhooks.

attribution.title: Attribution & MMPs
attribution.subtitle: Mobile measurement partners. AppsFlyer, Adjust, Branch, Singular.

ads.title: Ad networks & marketing
ads.subtitle: Server-side conversion APIs. Meta, Google, TikTok and more.

analytics.title: Product analytics
analytics.subtitle: Forward Rovenue revenue events into your analytics stack.

data.title: Data warehouses & CDPs
data.subtitle: Stream events into Snowflake, BigQuery, Redshift, S3.

lifecycle.title: CRM & lifecycle marketing
lifecycle.subtitle: Trigger lifecycle messages from subscription state.

communication.title: Communication & alerts
communication.subtitle: Real-time alerts to Slack, Discord, PagerDuty.

automation.title: Workflow automation
automation.subtitle: No-code workflow tools and project tracking.

identity.title: Auth & identity
identity.subtitle: Map auth provider user IDs to Rovenue subscribers.

billing.title: Billing sources
billing.subtitle: Where the money comes from — Stripe, Paddle, App Store, Play.

toolbar.searchPlaceholder: Search 50+ apps by name, category, or vendor…

featured.title: Pipe Apple Search Ads + Meta CAPI in two clicks
featured.description: Turn-key recipe: AppsFlyer + Meta Ads + Apple Search Ads, pre-mapped with subscription value, trial conversions, and refund clawbacks. Ships with a saved dashboard.

results.emptyHelp: Try a broader search or request a custom integration.

buildYourOwn.title: Build your own integration
buildYourOwn.description: Subscribe to any combination of Rovenue events from your own service. HMAC-signed payloads, configurable retries, replayable from the dashboard.

appsflyer.name: AppsFlyer
appsflyer.description: Mobile attribution & marketing analytics. Sync postbacks for installs, in-app purchases, and re-engagement.

adjust.name: Adjust
adjust.description: Attribution, fraud prevention, and audience builder. Forwards subscription events to your Adjust dashboard.

branch.name: Branch
branch.description: Deep linking + attribution platform. Map subscription LTV to acquisition channels.

singular.name: Singular
singular.description: Marketing analytics with cost aggregation across 2,500+ ad networks.

tenjin.name: Tenjin
tenjin.description: Mobile measurement for indie studios and growing apps.

meta-ads.name: Meta Ads
meta-ads.description: Send conversion events to Facebook & Instagram CAPI. Optimize for purchase and subscription value.

google-ads.name: Google Ads
google-ads.description: Send conversions and offline data via Enhanced Conversions API.

tiktok-ads.name: TikTok Ads
tiktok-ads.description: Forward purchase and subscription events to TikTok Events API for ROAS optimization.

snapchat-ads.name: Snapchat Ads
snapchat-ads.description: Pipe conversions to Snap Conversions API.

reddit-ads.name: Reddit Ads
reddit-ads.description: Server-side conversion API for Reddit advertising.

apple-search-ads.name: Apple Search Ads
apple-search-ads.description: iOS app store search attribution and post-install events.

linkedin-ads.name: LinkedIn Ads
linkedin-ads.description: B2B conversion tracking for sponsored campaigns.

amplitude.name: Amplitude
amplitude.description: Stream Rovenue revenue events into Amplitude for funnel and cohort analysis.

mixpanel.name: Mixpanel
mixpanel.description: Cohort analysis, retention, and behavioral analytics.

heap.name: Heap
heap.description: Auto-captured product analytics with a Rovenue revenue layer.

ga4.name: Google Analytics 4
ga4.description: GA4 Measurement Protocol — server-side revenue events.

firebase.name: Firebase Analytics
firebase.description: Mobile-first analytics with Rovenue subscription state.

segment.name: Segment
segment.description: Forward all Rovenue events through your Segment workspace to 300+ destinations.

snowflake.name: Snowflake
snowflake.description: Sync subscriptions, transactions, and ledger events into your Snowflake warehouse hourly.

bigquery.name: BigQuery
bigquery.description: Stream events into Google BigQuery for SQL analytics.

redshift.name: Amazon Redshift
redshift.description: Bulk + streaming sync to Redshift clusters.

s3.name: Amazon S3
s3.description: Hourly Parquet exports to your S3 bucket.

braze.name: Braze
braze.description: Trigger lifecycle messages on subscription milestones, churn risk, and trial conversions.

iterable.name: Iterable
iterable.description: Cross-channel marketing automation triggered by subscription events.

customerio.name: Customer.io
customerio.description: Trigger emails, push, and SMS based on Rovenue events.

hubspot.name: HubSpot
hubspot.description: Sync subscribers as contacts with revenue properties.

salesforce.name: Salesforce
salesforce.description: Push subscription events to SF Sales/Service Cloud.

slack.name: Slack
slack.description: Real-time alerts to Slack channels — milestones, anomalies, refunds.

pagerduty.name: PagerDuty
pagerduty.description: Trigger incidents on revenue anomalies and ingest failures.

intercom.name: Intercom
intercom.description: Show subscription state in your Intercom support inbox.

zendesk.name: Zendesk
zendesk.description: Surface customer revenue in support tickets.

linear.name: Linear
linear.description: Open issues for negative experiments and churn anomalies.

notion.name: Notion
notion.description: Push weekly metric snapshots into Notion databases.

auth0.name: Auth0
auth0.description: Map Auth0 user IDs to Rovenue subscribers.

firebase-auth.name: Firebase Auth
firebase-auth.description: Use Firebase user IDs as the Rovenue subscriber identity.

clerk.name: Clerk
clerk.description: Drop-in user management with Rovenue identity sync.

supabase.name: Supabase
supabase.description: Postgres + auth. Sync user IDs and subscription state.

stripe.name: Stripe
stripe.description: Source-of-truth for web payments. Two-way sync of customers, subscriptions, refunds.

paddle.name: Paddle
paddle.description: Merchant of record for SaaS. Handles tax globally.

lemonsqueezy.name: Lemon Squeezy
lemonsqueezy.description: Merchant of record platform for digital products.

apple-iap.name: Apple In-App Purchases
apple-iap.description: iOS, iPadOS, tvOS, macOS subscriptions and one-time purchases. App Store Server Notifications v2.

play-billing.name: Google Play Billing
play-billing.description: Android subscriptions and in-app purchases. Real-time developer notifications.

sdkApi.title: SDK & API
sdkApi.subtitle: Drop the Rovenue SDK into your app, manage project keys, and explore the public REST surface — versioned, signed, replayable.

hero.title: One SDK, every platform — production telemetry built in.
hero.description: Native StoreKit 2 + Play Billing 6 under the hood, JWS-verified server-side, and a single REST surface that everything else talks to. Track latency and success rate live across every install.
hero.healthcheckCaption: Returns 200 + region routing metadata. Use this in CI to gate deploys.

quickstart.title: Quickstart
quickstart.subtitle: Pick a target — install, configure, and read entitlements in under five minutes.
quickstart.installHint: Add the package to your project.
quickstart.initHint: Configure once at startup with your publishable key.

keys.title: Project keys
keys.subtitle: Publishable keys ship in your app. Secret keys stay on your servers — rotate any time without downtime.

footer.note: All key activity is hash-chained to the audit log.

packages.title: SDK packages
packages.subtitle: Open-source clients. Pin a version or follow the floating release channel.

targets.reactNative: Expo & bare React Native · iOS + Android

endpoints.title: REST endpoints
endpoints.subtitle: All requests authenticate with your secret key. Base URL: {{baseUrl}}

empty.title: No endpoints match
empty.body: Try a broader query or switch the scope filter.

webhooks.title: Webhooks
webhooks.subtitle: Server → server delivery with HMAC signing, automatic retries for 24h, and replay from the dashboard.
